plan: 1. Initialize npm project: Run `npm init -y` to create package.json with project name 'signal-intelligence' and set `"private": true`.
2. Install core dependencies: `npm install --save-dev vite typescript` — these are the only two required packages for this foundation ticket.

3. Create TypeScript config (tsconfig.json): Set target to ES2020, module to ESNext, moduleResolution to bundler, strict mode enabled, outDir to dist, include src/**/*.ts. These settings align with Vite's expectations.
4. Create Vite config (vite.config.ts): Minimal config with root set to default, build.outDir set to 'dist', and server port set to 5173 (Vite default).

5. Create the directory structure per CLAUDE.md: src/app/, src/layout/, src/signals/, src/permissions/, src/scoring/, src/modules/, src/ui/, src/styles/, tests/, public/. Only create the directories that need placeholder files for git tracking.

6. Create index.html at the project root (alongside vite.config.ts): Minimal HTML5 document with `<div id="app">` and `<script type="module" src="/src/app/main.ts">`. This file MUST be at the project root — NOT inside public/ — because Vite's HTML plugin only processes root-level index.html. Files in public/ are served as-is without transformation, so a <script> tag referencing a .ts source would not be bundled.
7. Create application entry files: src/app/main.ts (minimal bootstrap that renders a confirmation message to #app), src/styles/global.css (minimal reset imported by main.ts).
9. Add npm scripts to package.json: `"dev": "vite"`, `"build": "vite build"`, `"preview": "vite preview"`, `"typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"`.

10. Verify acceptance criteria: Run `npm install` (should succeed), `npm run build` (should produce dist/ with bundled JS), `npm run typecheck` (should pass with zero errors), then start `npm run dev` and confirm the default page loads at http://localhost:5173 without console errors.
testing_recommendations: Run `npm install` and confirm it completes with exit code 0 and no peer dependency errors.
Run `npm run build` and verify the dist/ directory is created with index.html and bundled JS assets.
Run `npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit) and confirm zero type errors.
Start `npm run dev`, open http://localhost:5173 in a browser, and confirm the page loads with the bootstrap message visible and zero console errors.
Verify `npm run preview` serves the production build from dist/ correctly.
risks: Vite and TypeScript version compatibility — use current stable versions (Vite 6.x, TypeScript 5.x) to avoid issues.

The CLAUDE.md specifies lint and test scripts (npm run lint, npm run test) but those tools (ESLint, Vitest) are not in scope for this ticket — the plan intentionally defers them to avoid scope creep. Future tickets must add them before any code review.
Node.js version on the developer's machine must support ES2020+ and modern Vite — Node 18+ is required.
